metadata.source_snapshot_note: Uploaded ZIP predates 9f07e00; the known narrow fixes are applied as canonical dispositions in this inventory.
metadata.status: Pass 2 complete; exact science replacement-text pass remains before audit v1.0.

C1-1.name: ISS Greenhouse Module
C1-1.estimated_gameplay_window: 8–12 min direct; 15–20 min with one cross-check per source
C1-1.primary_activity: Evidence triage: identify which observations are symptoms, which are controlled variables, and which identify the missing cue.
C1-1.best_optional_crosscheck: Return to Kim after collecting sensor, plant, and log evidence; her gated responses explicitly connect zero gravity, root morphology, and the historical record.
C1-1.likely_stumbling_point: Students may equate microgravity with “no forces” or assume roots simply grow toward water. The case also uses “zero g,” which should be corrected to microgravity/apparent weightlessness.
C1-1.cer: Claim which environmental cue is missing; cite the gravity reading and root pattern; explain gravitropism and why physical/light guides could substitute.
C1-1.exit_ticket: Why can a plant remain alive in microgravity while still growing in a disorganized way?
ngss_candidates: MS-ETS1-1 (supporting, if students define guide criteria)
C1-1.science_disposition: Core mechanism is sound. Edit “zero g” language and correct the Veggie first-harvest fun fact.

direct_routes.evidence: Roots fail to orient downward.
The habitat is in microgravity.
Roots form tangled mats; shoots orient poorly.
Gravity normally supplies a directional cue.

C1-2.name: Lunar Greenhouse
C1-2.estimated_gameplay_window: 8–12 min direct; 14–18 min with mechanical-pollination exploration
C1-2.primary_activity: Reproductive-process flowchart: flower → pollen release/movement → stigma contact → fruit set; mark the missing step.
C1-2.best_optional_crosscheck: After the pollen clue, read the hand/mechanical pollination log entries or try the gated stem-shaking interaction. These turn the solution into a testable mechanism rather than a vocabulary answer.
C1-2.likely_stumbling_point: Students may think “self-pollinating” means no pollen movement is needed, or assume every flowering crop uses the same mechanism.
C1-2.cer: Claim that pollination failure caused the missing fruit; cite undisturbed pollen, low airflow, and absent pollination plan; explain why healthy flowers alone are insufficient.
C1-2.exit_ticket: Why did extra nutrients or healthier leaves fail to solve this case?
ngss_candidates: MS-ETS1-2 (compare fans, vibration, or manual approaches)
C1-2.science_disposition: Core tomato-pollination mechanism is sound. Replace the unsupported 1990s bumblebee fun fact with NASA’s documented honeybee experiments (1982/1984).

direct_routes.evidence: Healthy flowers drop without fruit.
Air movement is nearly absent.
Mature pollen remains undisturbed.
No pollination plan was implemented.

C1-3.name: Mars Habitat
C1-3.estimated_gameplay_window: 9–13 min direct; 15–20 min with spectral cross-checks
C1-3.primary_activity: Spectrum comparison: separate total photon flux, wavelength distribution, pigment response, and observed tissue pattern.
C1-3.best_optional_crosscheck: Use the gated spectral analysis and fiber-optic search to compare total PAR with wavelength distribution. This is the case’s strongest conceptual move: quantity is not quality.
C1-3.likely_stumbling_point: The current mechanism teaches a real spectrum concept through a likely false engineering cause: Martian dust is described as preferentially removing red light, while NASA observations show dust storms transmitting red while strongly suppressing green and blue.
C1-3.cer: Claim that a spectrum mismatch—not total light or nutrients—caused the symptom; cite new-growth bleaching and the measured delivery system; explain why adequate PAR can still be biologically inadequate.
C1-3.exit_ticket: What is the difference between “enough light” and “the right light”?
C1-3.science_disposition: Major game-text edit required. Preserve the spectrum lesson but replace the Mars-dust/borosilicate red-loss mechanism with a measured equipment fault, filter/coating problem, or failed red LED channel. Soften the claim that green light is wasted.

direct_routes.evidence: New growth yellows first and ignores nutrient additions.
Light arrives through surface pipes plus white LEDs.
New tissue fails to develop chlorophyll.
The record attributes chlorophyll formation to needed wavelengths.

C1-4.name: Hayes Orbital Station
C1-4.estimated_gameplay_window: 9–13 min direct; 15–20 min with timeline cross-checks
C1-4.primary_activity: Timeline and variable isolation: distinguish the independent system change, delayed response, cellular damage, and downstream sensor fluctuations.
C1-4.best_optional_crosscheck: Build the change/crash timeline, then read the continuous-light and photooxidative-stress entries. The temporal sequence is stronger evidence than the manual’s overbroad biological claim.
C1-4.likely_stumbling_point: Students may learn the false universal rule that Spirulina necessarily dies without darkness. Continuous cultivation and illumination can work; damage depends on irradiance, culture density, mixing, temperature, and reactor control.
C1-4.cer: Claim that the lighting change exceeded the reactor/culture’s safe operating conditions; cite the change timeline and microscopy; explain photoinhibition/photooxidative stress without declaring darkness universally essential.
C1-4.exit_ticket: Why is the CO₂ fluctuation better treated as an effect of the crash than its original cause?
C1-4.science_disposition: Major game-text edit required. Reframe from “continuous light always causes collapse” to reactor-specific excessive continuous irradiance/insufficient light control; restoring a controlled cycle is one valid engineering fix, not a universal biological law.

direct_routes.action: Ask what happened and what changed.
direct_routes.evidence: Crashes began after the station switched to 24/0 light.
The reactor receives continuous corridor lighting.
Cells show photooxidative stress rather than infection.
The manual asserts a required dark period.

C1-5.name: Europa Outpost
C1-5.estimated_gameplay_window: 8–12 min direct; 15–20 min with evidence-convergence dialogue
C1-5.primary_activity: Risk-convergence matrix: hazard evidence, biological effect, failed protection, and eliminated alternatives.
C1-5.best_optional_crosscheck: The crew tree contains multiple pairwise and full-picture responses. Returning after two or three clues gives students explicit practice combining independent lines of evidence.
C1-5.likely_stumbling_point: The specific fictional dose can look more certain than it is, and the explanation incorrectly groups lunar bases with the ISS as protected by Earth’s magnetosphere.
C1-5.cer: Claim that inadequate radiation shielding caused repeated seedling failure; cite the radiation reading, DNA-damage pattern, and unvalidated shielding; explain why reproducing the result across seed batches points away from genetics.
C1-5.exit_ticket: Why is the construction log necessary if the sensor and microscope already point to radiation?
C1-5.science_disposition: Core radiation-risk principle is sound. Label the Europa dose and secondary-particle scenario as modeled fiction. Correct the Moon comparison: the ISS receives substantial geomagnetic protection; the lunar surface is largely exposed.

direct_routes.evidence: Every seed batch follows the same failure pattern.
The growth bay has an elevated ionizing-radiation reading.
Rapidly dividing tissues show damage consistent with DNA injury.
Shielding was not assessed for crop tolerance.

C1-6.name: First Contact Protocol
C1-6.estimated_gameplay_window: 13–17 min direct; 18–22 min with story/rapport exploration
C1-6.primary_activity: Systems-interruption map: scrubber input → removed compounds → failed coordination → dormancy.
C1-6.best_optional_crosscheck: Ask what changed at docking, then cross-reference the filtered compounds. This cleanly links a human engineering system to an unintended biological consequence.
C1-6.likely_stumbling_point: Students may anthropomorphize chemical signaling or interpret an alien mechanism as direct evidence that Earth plants consciously communicate.
C1-6.cer: Claim that atmospheric scrubbing disrupted the symbiosis; cite intact anatomy, missing volatiles, and docking timing; explain why “cleaner” air can be harmful to a different biological system.
C1-6.exit_ticket: Which part of the human habitat worked exactly as designed—and still caused the failure?
C1-6.science_disposition: Treat the alien network as fiction inspired by real VOC signaling and symbiosis. Teacher materials must distinguish chemical responses from intentional language or established “plant conversation.”

direct_routes.evidence: The organism depends on coordinated symbiosis.
Human scrubbers remove trace volatile compounds.
The network is intact but dormant.
The system coordinates through volatile signals.

C1-7.name: The Gift
C1-7.estimated_gameplay_window: 13–17 min direct; 18–22 min with solution-choice discussion
C1-7.best_optional_crosscheck: Compare extraction, synthesis, and living-network placement. This makes the case a compact culmination about mechanism, evidence, risk, and intervention.
C1-7.likely_stumbling_point: Students may collapse “a molecule can trigger germination” into “all seeds need nearby mature plants.” The implemented biology is alien fiction.
C1-7.cer: Claim that the pod requires a chemical germination cue; cite receptor readiness, missing VOCs, and archive evidence; explain why viability without germination indicates dormancy rather than damage.
C1-7.exit_ticket: What evidence proves that the pod is waiting rather than dead?
ngss_candidates: SEP: Constructing Explanations and Designing Solutions
C1-7.science_disposition: Keep as clearly labeled alien fiction. Use Earth VOC/germination research only as inspiration, noting that biological volatiles can stimulate or inhibit responses depending on organism and compound.

direct_routes.action: Ask why the pod must be near mature growth.
direct_routes.evidence: Established networks supply a missing context.
The isolation chamber lacks trace VOCs.
The viable pod is waiting for a chemical trigger.
A mature network releases the needed trigger compound.

C2-1.name: Vressk Centrifuge Habitat
C2-1.estimated_gameplay_window: 12–16 min direct; 17–20 min with species/cross-check dialogue
C2-1.primary_activity: Gradient model: sketch direction and relative magnitude at the top and bottom of the bed; annotate biological response.
C2-1.best_optional_crosscheck: Return to the Vressk botanist with each evidence type. The gated responses convert a physics fact into a species-specific design failure.
C2-1.likely_stumbling_point: Students may focus on the displayed average g and miss spatial variation. The case should not require formal algebra unless adapted for older learners.
C2-1.cer: Claim that nonuniform centrifugal acceleration caused the pattern; cite the profile and organ orientation; explain why matching average 2.1g is insufficient.
C2-1.exit_ticket: How can a system be “calibrated correctly” and still provide the wrong environment?
C2-1.science_disposition: The rotational gradient is real; species sensitivity and exact design tolerances are fictional. Treat large habitat-dimension claims as fictional engineering context unless separately sourced.

direct_routes.action: Ask what the crop is doing.
direct_routes.evidence: Tubers grow toward the ring wall.
Acceleration changes across bed depth.
Organs align with the local vector rather than intended rows.
The crop is adapted to a uniform native field.
Rotating habitats inherently produce radial gradients.

C2-2.name: Ares Botanical Garden
C2-2.estimated_gameplay_window: 12–16 min direct; 17–20 min with failed-solution analysis
C2-2.primary_activity: Failed-solution analysis: what was tried, what assumption it made, why it failed, and what mechanism the replacement must produce.
C2-2.best_optional_crosscheck: Use the hand-pollination failure as explicit disconfirming evidence. This teaches that a plausible solution can fail because it targets the wrong mechanism.
C2-2.likely_stumbling_point: Students may treat frequency as a magic number rather than one part of vibration amplitude, coupling, duration, and flower structure.
C2-2.cer: Claim that an acoustic/mechanical trigger is missing; cite trapped pollen, absent vibration, and failed hand transfer; explain buzz pollination.
C2-2.exit_ticket: What did the failed hand-pollination attempt teach that a successful attempt could not?
C2-2.science_disposition: Earth buzz pollination is well established. The Telluvian organism, exact frequency, and cultural history are fictional; avoid implying frequency alone determines release.

direct_routes.evidence: Buds abort and crucial knowledge was not shared clearly.
The expected vibration is absent.
Mature pollen remains trapped.
Contact-based hand pollination already failed.
Pollen release requires mechanical vibration.

C2-3.name: Oolian Mariculture Dome
C2-3.estimated_gameplay_window: 12–16 min direct; 17–20 min with action-spectrum comparison
C2-3.primary_activity: Action-spectrum matching: overlay available wavelengths with relative pigment absorption and identify the weakest overlap.
C2-3.best_optional_crosscheck: Compare old and new domes as a natural control. The “defective light” distractor becomes useful for separating equipment function from biological suitability.
C2-3.likely_stumbling_point: The current correct diagnosis says the kelp cannot use red light and the dome is functionally dark. Real brown algae still use chlorophyll a and transfer energy from chlorophyll c/fucoxanthin; the mismatch should be relative, not absolute.
C2-3.cer: Claim that the lamp spectrum is poorly matched to the organism; cite the spectrum, pigment profile, and old-dome comparison; explain adaptation to available light.
C2-3.exit_ticket: Why are the lights neither broken nor appropriate?
C2-3.science_disposition: Minor-to-moderate wording edit. Preserve the pigment lesson but replace absolutes (“cannot use red,” “effectively dark”) with low efficiency/poor spectral match.

direct_routes.evidence: Kelp declined only in the new Earth-lit dome.
The system is heavily red-weighted.
Pigments are adapted to blue-green-rich conditions.
The organism evolved in spectrally filtered deep water.
Different antenna pigments capture different spectral regions.

C2-4.name: Zhel’ii Diaspora Grove
C2-4.estimated_gameplay_window: 13–17 min direct; 18–22 min with callback synthesis
C2-4.primary_activity: Two-concept synthesis: create a chain linking schedule, internal timing, compound production, and network behavior.
C2-4.best_optional_crosscheck: Ask the caretaker about each clue after collection. The case is strongest when students explicitly combine the Campaign 1 lighting and signaling concepts.
C2-4.likely_stumbling_point: Students may overgeneralize the alien requirement into “all plants perform their most important work at night” or “stomata close at night in every plant.”
C2-4.cer: Claim that continuous light suppressed the fictional circadian signaling pathway; cite healthy tissue, absent compounds, and the timeline; explain why this is functional silence rather than damage.
C2-4.exit_ticket: Which evidence shows that this case is about timing rather than too much energy?
C2-4.science_disposition: Keep as species-specific alien fiction. Teacher notes should explain that Earth circadian responses and continuous-light tolerance vary widely; nighttime examples are illustrative, not universal.

direct_routes.evidence: Silence follows a recent management change.
The grove receives 24/0 light.
Tissue is healthy but signal compounds are absent.
The shift from 18/6 to 24/0 precedes silence.
The fictional network requires darkness to activate/reset signaling.

C2-5.name: Concord Botanical Vault
C2-5.estimated_gameplay_window: 13–17 min direct; 18–22 min with policy/solution choice
C2-5.primary_activity: Assumption and policy audit: identify the policy assumption, evidence that violates it, proposed exception, and safety constraints.
C2-5.best_optional_crosscheck: Compare a general safety policy with species-specific evidence. The case is an excellent assumption audit even if the Earth analogy is corrected.
C2-5.likely_stumbling_point: The current text presents radiation hormesis and fungal “radiosynthesis” as settled, direct analogues. Evidence exists for enhanced growth/metabolism in some melanized fungi under particular exposures, but the mechanism and generality are not equivalent to photosynthesis; human/plant hormesis is contested.
C2-5.cer: Claim that the fictional species requires a low-level radiation-triggered pathway; cite zero exposure, inactive pathway, and native conditions; clearly separate story biology from the tentative Earth analogy.
C2-5.exit_ticket: Why is “remove every hazard” not automatically a biologically neutral policy?
C2-5.science_disposition: Major qualification required. Label obligate radiation dependence as fiction. Rewrite the Earth parallel to say some experiments found radiation-enhanced growth/metabolism in melanized fungi under specific conditions; avoid stating established radiosynthesis or general radiation hormesis.

direct_routes.evidence: Medicinal output fails despite a seemingly perfect vault.
The chamber reports zero background radiation.
The DNA-repair-linked pathway is inactive.
The species evolved under high background radiation.
The database asserts fictional obligate radiation dependence.

C2-6.name: The First Garden
C2-6.estimated_gameplay_window: 14–18 min direct; 20+ min if the full Kess memory/rapport path is required
C2-6.primary_activity: Evidence-based restoration recommendation with separate science, intervention, regulation, risk, and uncertainty boxes.
C2-6.best_optional_crosscheck: The richer path is Kess’s fragmented-memory sequence plus the database’s mycorrhizal and biosafety entries. The shortest current path can reveal the mycorrhizal clue through a “database entries” prompt after reading only the legal-precedent clue; teacher materials should direct students to the biology entry before accepting the conclusion.
C2-6.likely_stumbling_point: The case turns a valid fungal-root mutualism into overly categorical claims about mature trees subsidizing seedlings, defense warnings, universal resource sharing, and inoculation as the single required restoration fix.
C2-6.cer: Claim that missing fungal-root connectivity is a plausible limiting factor; cite isolated chemical patches and soil history; explain the proposed mechanism while acknowledging uncertainty and evaluating the exemption.
C2-6.exit_ticket: Which part of the final recommendation is directly supported, which is inferred, and which remains uncertain?
C2-6.science_disposition: Major qualification required. Keep mycorrhizal mutualism and fungal nutrient acquisition as established. Present plant-to-plant transfer/benefit as context-dependent and debated. Correct the carbon fun fact to 13.12 Gt CO₂e allocated to mycorrhizal mycelium annually—not 13 billion tons of stored carbon or a 2023 global map.

direct_routes.action: Ask what restoration has already tried.
direct_routes.evidence: Chemistry and conventional repairs have been exhausted.

direct_routes.action: Ask what the chemical pattern shows.
direct_routes.evidence: Useful compounds remain isolated in patches.
Cross-zone inoculation is restricted.

direct_routes.action: After regulation is known, search precedents.
direct_routes.evidence: An exemption route exists.
The missing biological connection is identified as mycorrhizal.

serious_science_priorities.topic: Moon grouped with ISS geomagnetic protection
historical space-plant and bee fun facts
absolute statements about green/red light usability

cross_case_findings: Formal clue acquisition is shallow by design; curriculum rigor must come from interpretation and reasoning.
Every case has a purposeful evidence route compatible with approximately 20 minutes of gameplay.
Optional gated dialogue often contains the best cross-source reasoning but cannot be required.
Campaign 2 advances from missing-variable diagnosis to species-specific assumption and policy testing.
Game score must remain separate from academic assessment.
C2-6 curriculum should require reading the mycorrhizal biology entry before accepting the restoration recommendation.
